## auth_total_logins

Daily, CanadaLogin-wide sign-in totals across all applications.

Each row represents one day and summarizes overall CanadaLogin authentication
activity. Unlike [`app_login_counts`](#app_login_counts), there is no
per-application breakdown.

`Keywords`: sign-ins, logins, totals, CanadaLogin overall, unique users, daily

### Provenance

Queried directly from the IBM Verify production instance via API.

* **Updated**: Daily, at 6am America/Toronto
* **Steward**: EDCP Data and Research Team
* **Contact**: [Yinlin Zhang](mailto:yinlin.zhang@cds-snc.ca)

### Fields

One row per day.

| Field Name | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| from_date | timestamp | Start of the reporting day (midnight) |
| to_date | timestamp | End of the reporting day (23:59:59) |
| unique_users | integer | Number of distinct users who signed in to CanadaLogin that day. In IBM Verify, this is referred to as "unique logins". | 
| failed_logins | integer | Number of failed sign-in attempts that day |
| successful_logins | integer | Number of successful authentication events that day. |
| mtd_unique_users | integer | Cumulative distinct users month-to-date. Resets at the start of each month |
| ytd_unique_users | integer | Cumulative distinct users year-to-date. Resets at the start of each calendar year |
| rolling_365d_unique_users | integer | Distinct users who signed in to CanadaLogin in the trailing 365 days ending on that row's date |
| last_updated_at | timestamp | When the underlying data for this row was last updated |
| fetched_at | timestamp | When the aggregation last fetched this row |
| date | string | The reporting day as a text date (for example, "2026-05-21"). Mirrors `from_date` |

### Notes

`unique_users`, `failed_logins`, and `successful_logins` are daily values. The
`mtd_`, `ytd_`, and `rolling_365d_` unique-user fields are cumulative and should
not be summed across rows; take the latest value within the window instead.

`rolling_365d_unique_users` is null before 2026-06-13, its implementation date.

A `since_apr22_unique_users` field (cumulative distinct users since the
April 22, 2026 launch) was previously provided here. It has been removed from the
pipeline because the source API retains only the most recent 366.5 days of data,
so a since-launch cumulative count cannot be maintained once launch is more than
a year in the past. See the retention note on the
[IBM Verify catalog overview](#data-retention).

When a user signs in to CanadaLogin itself, IBM Verify records each
authentication method as a separate event. Entering a username and password
followed by a second factor like an OTP counts as two successful events. A
mistyped password that is then entered correctly, followed by a correct OTP,
counts as three events: two successful and one failed. Both `successful_logins`
and `failed_logins` are therefore inflated relative to the number of distinct
sign-in sessions.

This inflation applies only to signing in to CanadaLogin itself. Authenticating
against an integrated application does not inflate these counts, because the user
reuses the credentials already established in their CanadaLogin session and
enters no password or second factor (see [`app_login_counts`](#app_login_counts)).

Summing `successful_logins` is therefore a misleading indicator of actual user
engagement. Instead, use the `unique_users` field, which is daily active users,
and is a measure of the number of users who engaged with the system in a given
day.
